reasoning:
The base rate of an equity returning positively over short to medium terms is typically slightly above 50%, although it is often close to 50%. The current GEN closing price is 27.46 (as of 12-19). The statistical trend analysis gives an estimate P(> 27.46) of around 40% for the first resolution date, due to recent downward momentum and negative local trend. There was a recent insider sell on Dec 10, a dividend payout around Dec 10 which inherently slightly knocks down the price post-ex-dividend and can affect momentum (though ex-div date is already passed), and strong Q2 earnings announced in early November. This paints a picture of a decent fundamental backdrop (Q2 record results, Wall street upgrades) but poor very short-term momentum. 
For 2025-12-28 (1 week out), I predict 44% given the negative short term momentum and the standard 46% linear model estimate.
For 2026-01-20 (1 month out), momentum effects begin to decay, pushing the estimate towards 48%.
For 2026-03-21 (3 months out), predicting stock short-term technicals is very hard and the general long-term market drift (and base rate of market going up over 3-month periods) begins to dominate, pushing the probability slightly above 50% to roughly 51%.
